beats.narration: An automatic processor does the same four jobs as the manual line — develop, fix, wash, dry — but exactly the same way every time.

payload.title: THE MACHINE THAT NEVER FORGETS
payload.sub: Same four steps. Locked, not judged.

beats.narration: Notice what disappeared. Nobody picks a development time. Transport speed and rack length set it. Temperature is thermostatic; chemistry runs hot, near eighty Fahrenheit.

payload.heading: What the machine controls for you

beats.narration: Replenishment is metered, not guessed. A detector senses film entering and pumps fresh chemistry by area, so feeding habits matter.

payload.heading: Feed it right or the metering lies

beats.narration: But consistency cuts both ways: it repeats a bad state onto every film. So give it a physical each morning.
So the trade is clear. The processor buys you speed and films that match all shift — if you feed it, chart it, and clean it.
